HomePage.heading: Your résumé tells what you've done. MIRA tells who you are.
HomePage.subhead: Your MiraCard lets you apply to associations and get discovered by companies on the platform. One profile that keeps opening new opportunities.
HomePage.orgEyebrow: Are you a company or a university association?
HomePage.associationBody: Apply for your association: manage applications, board members, and your MIRA page.
HomePage.companyBody: Don't wait for applications. Find the right candidates: describe who you're looking for, MIRA shows you who really fits.

LoginPage.heading: Log in
LoginPage.pendingRequest: Your access request is still awaiting confirmation from the MIRA team. You'll get an email as soon as it's approved.
LoginPage.wrongAccountType: This is a {accountType} account. Switch to "{targetLabel}" above to sign in.
LoginPage.callbackFailed: This confirmation link was already used — this often happens because some email filters open it automatically before you do. If you received the email, your address is likely already confirmed: try logging in below.

SignupPage.heading: Create your account
SignupPage.emailHelper: Use your university email, not a personal one.
SignupPage.passwordHelper: At least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character.
SignupPage.selectUniversityAndDegree: Select your university and degree level.

AziendePage.formIntro: Fill in your details to request access to MIRA. Our team will review the request and, if approved, send you an email to create your account.
AziendePage.contactEmailHelper: If the request is approved, you'll receive the account creation link at this address.

errors.duplicate_pending_request: You already have a pending request with this email.
errors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

landing.heading: Discover the right talent before anyone else, beyond the résumé
landing.subhead: A résumé tells you what a student has done. MIRA also shows you who they are, what they're looking for, when they're available, and how well they fit the role.
landing.pilotNote: Pilot launching September 2026. Free initial access.

step1.title: Describe what you're looking for
step1.body: Write in natural language the profile you need: role, skills, sector, attitude, availability, and much more.

step2.title: MIRA finds the matches
step2.body: The system compares your search with student profiles and shows you the most relevant candidates, with an explanation for each.

step3.title: Contact and interviews
step3.body: Send a contact request to the student. If they accept, a private chat opens where you can get to know each other and, if there's mutual interest, arrange an interview.

landing.whyHeading: Why this isn't just another recruiting portal

row1.left: You receive applications and résumés to sift through
row1.right: No job postings to publish every time.

row2.left: Information limited to what's stated in the résumé
row2.right: Profiles enriched with experience, interests, attitude, availability, and goals

row3.left: You don't really know what's behind a candidate
row3.right: Define who you want and automatically discover the best-fitting candidates.

row4.left: The student only updates their profile when job hunting
row4.right: Every match comes with an explanation of why it fits

headerFields.annoInizio: the year you started your program

OnboardingEngine.introMessage:
Hi {firstName}! I'm MIRA.

Let's build your MIRA card together: you need it now to apply to associations, and it's the profile companies will use to find and contact you directly once you're a match for what they're looking for. The better it is, the harder it works for you.

Your MIRA card will be written in English, the format companies and associations expect.
OnboardingEngine.knownLivelloClosing: According to your registration, you're doing {livello}. Is that right?
OnboardingEngine.livelloQuestionIntro: Let's start with the basics: are you doing a bachelor's, master's, or single-cycle degree?
OnboardingEngine.livelloQuestionPlain: Are you doing a bachelor's, master's, or single-cycle degree?
OnboardingEngine.errorSaveLivello: Couldn't save your degree level — try again.
OnboardingEngine.errorHeaderRowNotFound: Header row not found for this student — try again.
OnboardingEngine.afterLivelloMagistrale: Great. Before we continue, where did you do your bachelor's, in what program, and with what final grade?
OnboardingEngine.afterLivelloOther: Upload your transcript as a PDF: I'll read your program, exams, grades, and average straight from it. If you don't have it handy, you can skip.
OnboardingEngine.previousDegreeQuestion: Where did you do your bachelor's, in what program, and with what final grade?
OnboardingEngine.errorSavePreviousDegree: Couldn't save your previous education — try again.
OnboardingEngine.afterPreviousDegree: Thanks. Now upload your master's transcript: I'll read your updated program, exams, grades, and average. If you don't have it handy, you can skip.
OnboardingEngine.skipTranscriptMessage: Okay — at least tell me the name of your program and what year you're in: you can upload your transcript anytime from your Profile, updating your average and exams.
OnboardingEngine.transcriptDoneWithMissing: Done: {courses} exams, {credits} credits{avg}. I just need {missing} — tell me and the card is ready to confirm.
OnboardingEngine.transcriptDoneComplete: Done: {courses} exams, {credits} credits{avg}. The card is ready — confirm the Header block on the right.
OnboardingEngine.errorSaveHeader: Couldn't save the Header — try again.
OnboardingEngine.headerGapComplete: Great, the card is ready — confirm the Header block on the right to continue.
OnboardingEngine.afterHeaderApproved: Do you have a CV? Upload it and I'll use it to ask targeted questions about your experiences. If you don't have one, no problem: we'll build it by talking.
OnboardingEngine.hiddenExperienceQuestion: Is there something you've never put on your CV? Personal projects, competitions, competitive sports, volunteering, jobs — anything you think represents you.
OnboardingEngine.skipCVMessage: No problem, we'll build it by talking. {hiddenExperienceQuestion}
OnboardingEngine.cvItemGood:
From your CV: "{title} @ {org}" — {description}

Does that look right, or do you want to add something?
OnboardingEngine.cvFoundOne: I found one experience. On {title} @ {org} — what did you actually do?
OnboardingEngine.cvFoundMany: I found {count} experiences. On {title} @ {org} — what did you actually do?
OnboardingEngine.cvNextPlain: On {title} @ {org} — what did you actually do?
OnboardingEngine.esperienzeDone: Written up — check the Experiences block on the right. Confirm it when you're ready.
OnboardingEngine.afterEsperienzeApproved: One last thing to unlock applications: do you have work availability to share, so companies can find you when you're a match? Tell me what you're looking for — an internship in a specific field, a part-time role, a project — and from when: an open-ended date ("from September onward") or a precise period ("from June to August") both work. Feel free to mention a location preference too. If you're not currently looking or already committed elsewhere, just say so: that's fine too.
OnboardingEngine.disponibilitaQuestion: What are you looking for (an internship, part-time, a project...) and from when are you available?
OnboardingEngine.disponibilitaDone: Noted — check the Availability block. Confirm it to unlock applications.
OnboardingEngine.afterDisponibilitaApproved: Applications unlocked. Your card is at {pct}%: you can already apply to associations on MIRA. Or we can finish the last sections — skills, interests, how you describe yourself, career plan — in about 5 minutes: associations see a much stronger profile.
OnboardingEngine.startFaseBMessage:
Let's finish the last sections together: a few minutes and your card is done.

From your grades, here are the academic skills I'd already suggest:
{listText}

Now tell me: what tools, software, or methods did you use in the experiences you told me about?
OnboardingEngine.noCompetenzeDraft: (no draft — you can add some from your Profile later)
OnboardingEngine.softSkillsIntro: Now a few quick scenarios — pick whichever sounds more like you. There are no right answers: these just tell companies how you work, not who's better.

q1.prompt: Group project at university, presentation is tomorrow. What usually happens?
q1.optionA: I present: talking in front of people doesn't bother me — I actually enjoy it.
q1.optionB: I prepare the slides and the content, but I'd rather someone else did the presenting.

q2.prompt: Your team needs information from a company or professor none of you knows. How do you handle it?
q2.optionA: I reach out myself: making first contact with people I don't know comes easily to me.
q2.optionB: I'd rather someone else made the first move — I do my best work on the material itself, not on the phone with a stranger.

q3.prompt: You have two weeks for an important deadline. How do you approach it?
q3.optionA: I make a plan on day one — a rough outline and milestones — and stick to it.
q3.optionB: I start right away and adjust as I go: I close out the bulk of it under deadline pressure, and that works well for me.

q4.prompt: It's the night before the deadline and the project requirements change. What's your honest reaction?
q4.optionA: It's annoying, but I quickly rebuild the plan and get back on track in an organized way.
q4.optionB: It energizes me — reorganizing everything on the fly is when I perform best.

q5.prompt: You're working on a project or a piece of coursework. What does your ideal day look like?
q5.optionA: Working side by side with others: bouncing ideas back and forth constantly energizes me.
q5.optionB: Working alone and focused, then syncing up with the team at key moments.

OnboardingEngine.competenzeSequenceDone: Here are your skills — take a look on the right and confirm them when they look good.
OnboardingEngine.errorSaveCompetenzeProposed: Couldn't save the suggested skills — try again.
OnboardingEngine.errorCompetenzeRowNotFound: Skills row not found for this student — try again.
OnboardingEngine.errorSaveCompetenze: Couldn't save the skills — try again.
OnboardingEngine.competenzeAdded: Added — check the skills on the right. Confirm them when they look good.
OnboardingEngine.competenzeNotLinked: I couldn't link it to a specific exam or experience — you can add it directly in the Skills block on the right, noting what it refers to.
OnboardingEngine.lingueQuestion: What languages do you know, and at what level? (If your program is in English, mention that too alongside the others.)
OnboardingEngine.interessiIntroQuestion:
Now for a part that's usually missing from a CV, but that companies actually read closely: what you're genuinely interested in. Not a list of words like 'finance and travel' — everyone writes that. I'll help with three questions:
— What do you follow on your own, outside of your coursework? Newsletters, podcasts, channels, topics you always end up reading about. And is there a topic that, when it comes up, you talk about more than you probably should?
— Outside of school: what do you do consistently? Sports, music, projects, anything — unusual stuff counts too, actually that's what people remember. And for how long?
— Bonus: is there something you're oddly good at that has nothing to do with university?
Write freely, even messily — I'll shape it, then you approve. Only one thing matters: things you actually do, not things that sound good.
OnboardingEngine.autodescrizioneIntroQuestion:
Now the part that makes your card different from everyone else's: two lines about who you are, written by you. No interview tone — everyone knows how to write that, which is exactly why they all sound the same. If you don't know where to start:
— What would your friends say about you, if you actually asked them? (usually more accurate than what we'd say ourselves)
— What can't you stand, at work or in your studies? That says something about you too.
— Is there one thing you won't give up? Sports, family, a project of your own — priorities matter.
— What does a day look like when you go home satisfied?
Write the way you talk, even if it's messy — I'll shape it, then you approve. And be honest: this part only works if it's true. Whoever reads it wants to understand if you're the right person, not find the perfect candidate.
OnboardingEngine.pianoCarrieraMagistrale:
Last section, and for companies it's one of the most useful: where you're headed. You don't need to have it figured out — just be honest about where you stand. I'll help:
— Over the next few years, do you have a clear direction (a sector, a role), a few hypotheses you're weighing, or are you still exploring? All three answers are fine, genuinely.
— Is there already something on the calendar or on your mind? An exchange, a certification, a project of your own.
— And your next work experience: what would you want it to leave you with? Even just 'figure out if consulting is for me' is a useful answer.
Write it however it comes — I'll shape it, you approve. One tip: don't write the goal that sounds ambitious if it's not really yours. 'I'm exploring, X and Y interest me' tells a company much more than a fake 'I want to do investment banking'.
OnboardingEngine.pianoCarrieraOther:
Last section, and for companies it's one of the most useful: where you're headed. You don't need to have it figured out — just be honest about where you stand. I'll help:
— Over the next few years, do you have a clear direction (a sector, a role), a few hypotheses you're weighing, or are you still exploring? All three answers are fine, genuinely.
— Is there already something on the calendar or on your mind? An exchange, a master's (where, if you know), a certification, a project of your own.
— And your next work experience: what would you want it to leave you with? Even just 'figure out if consulting is for me' is a useful answer.
Write it however it comes — I'll shape it, you approve. One tip: don't write the goal that sounds ambitious if it's not really yours. 'I'm exploring, X and Y interest me' tells a company much more than a fake 'I want to do investment banking'.
OnboardingEngine.resumeFaseBMessage:
Welcome back! Let's finish the last sections of your card together.

{question}
OnboardingEngine.lingueDone: Noted — check the Languages block. Confirm it when it looks good.
OnboardingEngine.interessiDone: Written up — check the Interests block. Confirm it when it looks good.
OnboardingEngine.autodescrizioneDone: I wrote it in your own words — feel free to edit it, it's the most personal part of the card. Confirm it when it looks good.
OnboardingEngine.pianoCarrieraDone: Noted — no problem if you haven't decided yet, that's a valid answer too. Confirm the Career plan block to finish your card.
OnboardingEngine.chiusuraMessage: Your card is complete! You'll find it in your Profile, editable anytime. Good luck with your applications.
